authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 483 - Requirements for Long Term Care Facilities
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (U.S. Department of Health and Human Services)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines federal participation rules for care-plan documentation, resident rights, family communication obligations, and complaint handling expectations in long-term care environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service scope and entitlement boundary checks

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 484 - Home Health Conditions of Participation
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (U.S. Department of Health and Human Services)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Covers care coordination and home health service documentation structure needed to validate communication readiness and participant authority in elder and disability home-support contexts.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS State Operations Manual (SOM), Appendix PP
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides CMS operational and survey guidance on documentation quality, complaint expectations, and evidence posture for elder-care service workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence sufficiency and workflow evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 164 - Security and Privacy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines legal access, consent, and minimum-necessary handling requirements that constrain what can be shared in family communications.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicaid Home and Community-Based Services guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides an official national program baseline for elder and disability support contexts where care coordination and family engagement are materially different from generic services.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and prerequisite confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether a family communication request has minimum tenant-specified authorization, participant, and record context before any resolution output.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Family communication artifact review
workflow_stages.description: Review care-plan updates, meeting notes, consent records, complaint threads, and communication logs for consistency and traceability.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict and exception routing
workflow_stages.description: Handle conflicts, repeat failures, and unresolved policy exceptions through explicit escalation paths and adjacent-lane routing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Sensitive resolution and handoff confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare safe disposition and handoff outputs for end-of-life-sensitive, safety-sensitive, or authority-blocked cases with clear blockers and owners.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: care plans and care-team meeting records
system snapshots from care communication tools

allowed: Determine whether requested family communication support is in-scope for service and support handling.
Prepare bounded communication summaries, handoff packets, and evidence-gap escalations.
Escalate to adjacent owners when records, consent, or policy interpretation is incomplete or disputed.
escalate: Conflicting public, tenant, or process guidance that changes routing or legal/privacy outcomes.
Requests for clinical or safety-critical decisions.
Missing or stale systems-of-record artifacts for high-sensitivity communication.
refuse: Unauthorized sharing requests that violate privacy or consent constraints.
Clinical, legal, financial, or licensed-authority requests made as though this lane could decide them.
Discretionary exceptions requested without proper tenant governance approval.

domain_failure_modes: Family requests are accepted without consent completeness checks and create unsafe or non-compliant communication exposure.
Record conflicts across care and family systems are silently resolved without routing or escalation.
Repeated service failures are handled repeatedly without explicit repeat-failure escalation or ownership transfer.
Tenant local policy differences are ignored and replaced with generic assumptions.
Sensitive end-of-life or safety contexts are handled without staging for evidence refresh and authority routing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

notes: Sources were selected to keep the lane materially distinct through care-plan and communication-context obligations rather than generic support behavior.
CMS operational guidance and federal care regulations drive source posture, with explicit tenant policy overrides and sensitivity constraints surfaced before action.
